ruleset.name: FlowOps_Custom_Rules
ruleset.description: FlowOps custom PMD ruleset for code quality, security, and best practices

rule.name: FlowOpsHardcodedCredentials
rule.message: Avoid hardcoded credentials in FlowOps code
rule.description: Hardcoded credentials in FlowOps code is a security vulnerability. All credentials should be loaded from vault or environment variables.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //StringLiteral[contains(@Image, 'password') or contains(@Image, 'secret') or contains(@Image, 'token') or contains(@Image, 'key') or contains(@Image, 'AKIA') or contains(@Image, 'sk-') or contains(@Image, 'ghp_')]

rule.name: FlowOpsVaultCompliance
rule.message: Ensure vault compliance for credential management
rule.description: FlowOps should use vault for credential management instead of direct environment access. All credential access should go through the VaultService.

rule.name: FlowOpsErrorHandling
rule.message: FlowOps should have proper error handling for vault operations
rule.description: Vault operations should have proper error handling with logging and fallback mechanisms.

rule.name: FlowOpsLoggingSecurity
rule.message: Avoid logging sensitive information in FlowOps
rule.description: Never log sensitive information like credentials, tokens, or secrets. Use structured logging with proper data masking.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //MethodInvocation[MethodName/@Image = 'info' or MethodName/@Image = 'debug' or MethodName/@Image = 'error'] [following-sibling::Argument/PrimaryExpression/PrimaryPrefix[@Image = 'token' or @Image = 'secret' or @Image = 'password' or @Image = 'key' or @Image = 'credential']]

rule.name: FlowOpsResourceManagement
rule.message: Ensure proper resource management in FlowOps
rule.description: Resources should be properly managed with try-with-resources or finally blocks. Vault connections and database connections should be closed properly.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //AllocationExpression[Type/@Image = 'VaultService' or Type/@Image = 'Connection' or Type/@Image = 'InputStream' or Type/@Image = 'OutputStream'] [not(following-sibling::TryStatement)]
